Question: For functions $$f ( x ) = 3 x - 2$$ and $$g ( x ) = 5 x + 1 ,$$ find ⓐ $$( f \vysmwhtcircle g ) ( x )$$ ⓑ $$( g \vysmwhtcircle f ) ( x )$$ ⓒ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( x )$$ .
For functions $$f ( x ) = 4 x - 3 ,$$ and $$g ( x ) = 6 x - 5 ,$$ find ⓐ $$( f \vysmwhtcircle g ) ( x ) ,$$ ⓑ $$( g \vysmwhtcircle f ) ( x ) ,$$ and ⓒ $$( f \cdotp g ) ( x ) .$$
For functions $$f ( x ) = x^{2} - 9 ,$$ and $$g ( x ) = 2 x + 5 ,$$ find ⓐ $$( f \vysmwhtcircle g ) ( -2 ) ,$$ ⓑ $$( g \vysmwhtcircle f ) ( -3 ) ,$$ and ⓒ $$( f \vysmwhtcircle f ) ( 4 ) .$$
For functions $$f ( x ) = x^{2} + 1 ,$$ and $$g ( x ) = 3 x - 5 ,$$ find ⓐ $$( f \vysmwhtcircle g ) ( -1 ) ,$$ ⓑ $$( g \vysmwhtcircle f ) ( 2 ) ,$$ and ⓒ $$( f \vysmwhtcircle f ) ( -1 ) .$$

For each set of ordered pairs, determine if it represents a function and if so, is the function one-to-one. ⓐ $$\{ ( -3 , -6 ) , ( -2 , -4 ) , ( -1 , -2 ) , ( 0 , 0 ) , ( 1 , 2 ) , ( 2 , 4 ) , ( 3 , 6 ) \}$$ ⓑ $$\{ ( -4 , 8 ) , ( -2 , 4 ) , ( -1 , 2 ) , ( 0 , 0 ) , ( 1 , 2 ) , ( 2 , 4 ) , ( 4 , 8 ) \}$$

For each set of ordered pairs, determine if it represents a function and if so, is the function one-to-one. ⓐ $$\{ ( 27 , -3 ) , ( 8 , -2 ) , ( 1 , -1 ) , ( 0 , 0 ) , ( 1 , 1 ) , ( 8 , 2 ) , ( 27 , 3 ) \}$$ ⓑ $$\{ ( 7 , -3 ) , ( -5 , -4 ) , ( 8 , 0 ) , ( 0 , 0 ) , ( -6 , 4 ) , ( -2 , 2 ) , ( -1 , 3 ) \}$$
Answer: ⓐ Not a function ⓑ Function; not one-to-one

Question: Determine whether each graph is the graph of a function and, if so, whether it is one-to-one.
Determine whether each graph is the graph of a function and, if so, whether it is one-to-one.
Find the inverse of $$\{ ( 0 , 4 ) , ( 1 , 7 ) , ( 2 , 10 ) , ( 3 , 13 ) \} .$$ Determine the domain and range of the inverse function.
Find the inverse of $$\{ ( -1 , 4 ) , ( -2 , 1 ) , ( -3 , 0 ) , ( -4 , 2 ) \} .$$ Determine the domain and range of the inverse function.
Graph, on the same coordinate system, the inverse of the one-to one function.
Graph, on the same coordinate system, the inverse of the one-to one function.
Verify that the functions are inverse functions. $$f ( x ) = 4 x - 3$$ and $$g ( x ) = \frac{x + 3}{4} .$$
Answer: $$g ( f ( x ) ) = x ,$$ and $$f ( g ( x ) ) = x ,$$ so they are inverses.

Question: Verify that the functions are inverse functions. $$f ( x ) = 2 x + 6$$ and $$g ( x ) = \frac{x - 6}{2} .$$
Answer: $$g ( f ( x ) ) = x ,$$ and $$f ( g ( x ) ) = x ,$$ so they are inverses.

Question: Find the inverse of the function $$f ( x ) = 5 x - 3 .$$
Find the inverse of the function $$f ( x ) = 8 x + 5 .$$
Find the inverse of the function $$f ( x ) = \sqrt[5]{3 x - 2} .$$
Find the inverse of the function $$f ( x ) = \sqrt[4]{6 x - 7} .$$
